description: Curated editable Hebrew alphabet metadata for word inspection, gematria, and traditional symbolic notes.

letters.name: Aleph
letters.description: A silent guttural consonant; traditionally associated with strength and beginning.

letters.name: Bet
letters.description: A labial consonant that can be hard or soft; traditionally associated with a house.

letters.name: Gimel
letters.description: A consonant traditionally linked with the image of a camel and movement.

letters.name: Dalet
letters.description: A consonant traditionally associated with a door or pathway.

letters.name: He
letters.description: A consonant often used as a vowel letter at word endings; traditionally linked with breath or disclosure.

letters.name: Vav
letters.description: A consonant and vowel letter; traditionally associated with a hook or connector.

letters.name: Zayin
letters.description: A z-sound consonant with traditional associations of a weapon or provision.

letters.name: Het
letters.description: A strong guttural consonant traditionally associated with enclosure or life.

letters.name: Tet
letters.description: An emphatic t-sound consonant with traditional associations of what is enclosed or hidden.

letters.name: Yod
letters.description: A small consonant and vowel letter traditionally linked with the hand and action.

letters.name: Kaf
letters.description: A consonant with a final form; traditionally associated with the palm of the hand.

letters.name: Lamed
letters.description: An l-sound consonant traditionally associated with a goad and instruction.

letters.name: Mem
letters.description: A consonant with a final form; traditionally associated with water.

letters.name: Nun
letters.description: A consonant with a final form; traditionally associated with fish or continuation.

letters.name: Samekh
letters.description: An s-sound consonant traditionally linked with support.

letters.name: Ayin
letters.description: A guttural consonant traditionally associated with the eye and seeing.

letters.name: Pe
letters.description: A consonant with a final form; traditionally associated with the mouth and speech.

letters.name: Tsade
letters.description: A consonant with a final form; traditionally associated with a hook or righteous pursuit.

letters.name: Qof
letters.description: A q-sound consonant traditionally associated with what is behind or set apart.

letters.name: Resh
letters.description: An r-sound consonant traditionally associated with a head or chief.

letters.name: Shin/Sin
letters.description: A consonant whose dot distinguishes shin from sin; traditionally associated with a tooth.

letters.name: Tav
letters.description: A t-sound consonant traditionally associated with a mark or sign.

letters.name: Kaf Sofit
letters.description: The final form of Kaf used at the end of a word; counted as 500 in final-form gematria.

letters.name: Mem Sofit
letters.description: The final form of Mem used at the end of a word; counted as 600 in final-form gematria.

letters.name: Nun Sofit
letters.description: The final form of Nun used at the end of a word; counted as 700 in final-form gematria.

letters.name: Pe Sofit
letters.description: The final form of Pe used at the end of a word; counted as 800 in final-form gematria.

letters.name: Tsade Sofit
letters.description: The final form of Tsade used at the end of a word; counted as 900 in final-form gematria.
